rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned on default-mechanism questions: the 2025 citing paper explicitly tests whether default effects are passive/heuristic versus valuation-attention driven, while the 2020 cited paper’s core claim is that default acceptance is a more passive process in sequential choice. That makes the citation plausibly a key theory/mechanism source or direct comparison point rather than an incidental mention, so it should be retained for full relation judgment.
